title: Meditations II: Impermanence
description: The emperor-philosopher on the brevity of life, the smallness of human affairs, and the duty to act justly regardless.
introduction: The emperor-philosopher on the brevity of life, the smallness of human affairs, and the duty to act justly regardless.

translation.original_text: κἂν τρισχίλια ἔτη μέλλῃς βιώσεσθαι καὶ τοσαυτάκις μύρια, ὅμως μέμνησο ὅτι οὐδεὶς ἄλλον ἀποβάλλει βίον ἢ τοῦτον ὃν ζῇ.
translation.text: Even if you were going to live three thousand years, or three million, still remember that no one loses any other life than the one now being lived.
